---
package: "openpipeline_spatial"
version: "v0.5.0"
title: "spaceranger_count"
namespace: "Mapping"
description: "Count gene expression and protein expression reads from a single capture area."
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline_spatial v0.5.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `spaceranger_count`  
Namespace: `mapping`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial/tree/v0.5.0/src/mapping/spaceranger_count)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/mapping/spaceranger_count)

:::



## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/mapping/spaceranger_count/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline_spatial/v0.5.0/components/mapping/spaceranger_count).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline_spatial \
  -r v0.5.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/mapping/spaceranger_count/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name              |Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |Attributes                                                                                                                   |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--gex_reference` |Path of folder containing 10x-compatible reference                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |`file`, required, example: `"/path/to/refdata-gex-GRCh38-2020-A"`                                                            |
|`--input`         |The fastq.gz files to align. Can also be a single directory containing fastq.gz files.  Individual FASTQ files should follow the naming convention of 10x Genomics: [Sample Name]_S[Sample Number]_L[Lane Number]_[Read Type]_001.fastq.gz  Where: [Sample Name] is the name assigned during sample preparation/sequencing S[Sample Number] is the sample index (usually S1, S2, etc.) L[Lane Number] identifies the sequencing lane (L001, L002, etc.)  [Read Type] will be one of: R1 - Read 1 (contains the spatial barcode and UMI) R2 - Read 2 (contains the actual cDNA sequence) |List of `file`, required, example: `"sample_S1_L001_R1_001.fastq.gz", "sample_S1_L001_R2_001.fastq.gz"`, multiple_sep: `";"` |
|`--probe_set`     |CSV file specifying the probe set used                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |`file`, required, example: `"Visium_Human_Transcriptome_Probe_Set_v2.0_GRCh38-2020-A.csv"`                                   |
|`--cytaimage`     |Brightfield image generated by the CytAssist instrument.  When using CytAssist workflow, either this or --image must be provided.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |`file`, example: `"cyta_image.tif"`                                                                                          |
|`--image`         |H&E or fluorescence microscope image in TIFF or JPG format.  Required for standard Visium workflow, optional when using --cytaimage for CytAssist workflow.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |`file`, example: `"brightfield.tif"`                                                                                         |


### Outputs

|Name       |Description                               |Attributes                                     |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output` |The folder to store the alignment results |`file`, required, example: `"/path/to/output"` |


### Slide Information

|Name              |Description                                                                                                                                                                                                 |Attributes                            |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--slide`         |Visium slide serial number (e.g., 'V10J25-015')                                                                                                                                                             |`string`, example: `"V10J25-015"`     |
|`--area`          |Visium capture area identifier (e.g., 'A1')                                                                                                                                                                 |`string`, example: `"A1"`             |
|`--unknown_slide` |Use this option if the slide serial number and area were entered incorrectly on the CytAssist  instrument and the correct values are unknown. Not compatible with --slide, --area, or  --slide-file options |`string`                              |
|`--slidefile`     |Slide design file for offline use                                                                                                                                                                           |`file`, example: `"slide_design.gpr"` |
|`--override_id`   |Overrides the slide serial number and capture area provided in the Cytassist image metadata                                                                                                                 |`boolean_true`                        |


### Image Options

|Name                |Description                                                              |Attributes                                    |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--darkimage`       |Multi-channel, dark-background fluorescence image                        |`file`, example: `"fluorescence.tif"`         |
|`--colorizedimage`  |Color image representing pre-colored dark-background fluorescence images |`file`, example: `"colored_fluorescence.tif"` |
|`--dapi_index`      |Index of DAPI channel (1-indexed) of fluorescence image                  |`integer`, example: `1`                       |
|`--image_scale`     |Microns per microscope image pixel                                       |`double`, example: `0.65`                     |
|`--reorient_images` |Whether to rotate and mirror image to align fiducial pattern             |`boolean`, default: `TRUE`                    |


### Processing Options

|Name                |Description                                                                                                                            |Attributes                           |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--create_bam`      |Enable or disable BAM file generation                                                                                                  |`boolean`, required, default: `TRUE` |
|`--nosecondary`     |Disable secondary analysis (e.g., clustering)                                                                                          |`boolean_true`                       |
|`--r1_length`       |Hard trim the input Read 1 to this length before analysis                                                                              |`integer`                            |
|`--r2_length`       |Hard trim the input Read 2 to this length before analysis                                                                              |`integer`                            |
|`--filter_probes`   |Whether to filter the probe set using the "included" column                                                                            |`boolean`, default: `TRUE`           |
|`--custom_bin_size` |Bin Visium HD data to specified size in microns (4-100, even values only) in addition to the standard binning size (2 µm, 8 µm, 16 µm) |`integer`                            |


### Input Selection

|Name        |Description                               |Attributes                                                 |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--project` |Project folder name within mkfastq output |`string`                                                   |
|`--sample`  |Prefix of FASTQ filenames to select       |`string`                                                   |
|`--lanes`   |Only use FASTQs from selected lanes       |List of `integer`, example: `1, 2, 3`, multiple_sep: `";"` |

## Authors


  * Jakub Majercik [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/jakubmajercik) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/jakubmajercik) (author)
